title: Error -6 during image processing on iX1600
causes: Corrupted ScanSnap Home environment or local processing cache may cause image processing failure
solution_steps: Clean uninstall ScanSnap Home, run SSHomeClean.exe, reboot, reinstall latest ScanSnap Home as administrator, then retest scan processing
result_note: ScanSnap Home environment repaired or issue remains for further diagnostics

title: iX1600 loses USB and Wi-Fi connection repeatedly after firmware update
causes: Windows component corruption or communication instability may affect USB and Wi-Fi communication
solution_steps: Run SFC/DISM repair sequence, reboot between steps, then retest ScanSnap Home and scanner communication
result_note: Windows communication environment repaired or issue remains for next step

title: Unexpected error after firmware update on iX1600
causes: Firmware update may not have completed correctly and scanner may require model-specific recovery
solution_steps: Confirm scanner boot state and USB detection first; if recovery symptoms are confirmed, use iX1600 Top Sensor + Empty Arm recovery with standalone firmware updater via USB
result_note: Firmware recovery can restore normal boot if low-level recovery is required

title: ScanSnap Home startup crash with tb21 error
causes: Damaged ScanSnap Home installation or configuration data may prevent startup
solution_steps: Run cleanup tool, restart Windows, reinstall ScanSnap Home as administrator, then retest startup
result_note: Startup environment repaired or additional Windows repair required

title: Scanner not detected over USB
causes: Stale USB/ScanSnap device registration or USB enumeration issue may block detection
solution_steps: Check direct USB first; if pure USB enumeration issue persists, remove old USB/ScanSnap entries from Device Manager and rebuild USB stack
result_note: Scanner should be detected after reboot/reconnect if stale registration was the cause

title: Firmware update stuck or not completing
causes: USB communication interrupted during update, or scanner stuck in partial update state
solution_steps: Connect via direct USB, retry normal firmware update via ScanSnap Home; if scanner shows orange LED or logo-stuck state, proceed to model-specific recovery
result_note: Normal retry resolves most stuck updates; recovery gate required only if boot state is abnormal

title: Wi-Fi connection drops repeatedly
causes: Network environment instability or Windows communication component issue
solution_steps: Check direct USB first to isolate Wi-Fi issue; run SFC/DISM if Windows communication instability suspected; verify router channel and firewall settings
result_note: USB test isolates whether issue is Wi-Fi-specific or system-wide

title: ScanSnap Home crashes or hangs on startup
causes: Corrupted ScanSnap Home installation files or configuration database
solution_steps: Run SSHomeClean.exe, reboot, reinstall latest ScanSnap Home as administrator
result_note: Fresh install resolves most startup corruption issues

title: Scanner shows error code on display
causes: Hardware or firmware error indicated by scanner LED or display code
solution_steps: Note the exact error code/LED pattern; check scanner boot state via USB; if communication possible, run SSHomeClean and firmware check; if not, assess recovery need
result_note: Error code identification is critical for correct escalation path

title: Scanner not detected on macOS
causes: macOS system extension or USB communication issue blocking scanner recognition
solution_steps: Check direct USB connection; verify system extension permissions in macOS Security settings; run SSHomeClean and reinstall ScanSnap Home
result_note: macOS permission changes after updates frequently cause detection issues
